name: Fresh Ginger Muffins
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Lemon zest (with some white pith)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Grease the muffin tins.
3. Cut the unpeeled ginger into fine pieces (about 1/4 cup). It's better to have too much ginger than too little.
4. Place the ginger and 1/4 cup sugar in a small skillet or pan. Cook over medium heat until the sugar has melted and the mixture is hot. Do not leave the pan unattended—this cooking takes only a couple of minutes. Remove from the stove and let the ginger mixture cool.
5. In a food processor, combine the lemon zest and 3 tablespoons sugar. Process until the lemon peel is in small bits; or chop the lemon zest and pith by hand, then add the sugar.
6. Add the lemon mixture to the ginger mixture. Stir well.
7. In a mixing bowl, beat the butter for about a second. Add the remaining 1/2 cup sugar and beat until smooth. Add the eggs and beat well. Then add the buttermilk and mix until blended.
8. Add the flour, salt, and baking soda to the bowl. Beat until smooth.
9. Add the ginger-lemon mixture and mix well.
10. Spoon the batter into the muffin tins so that each cup is about 3/4 full.
11. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes.
12. Serve warm.
